concept.title: CI/CD Publishing Pipelines
concept.shortdesc: Automate DITA builds with GitHub Actions, Jenkins, and Azure DevOps so every commit produces validated, branded deliverables — no manual builds required.

section.title: Why automate DITA builds?

ul.li: Writers wait for a build engineer to produce outputs.
Build parameters drift between machines — "it works on my laptop."
Broken links and validation errors are discovered late.
Publishing becomes a ceremony instead of a continuous process.

section.p: CI/CD pipelines solve these problems by running DITA-OT automatically on every push, pull request, or release tag — validating content, building outputs, and deploying deliverables without human intervention.

section.title: Pipeline architecture
section.p: A typical DITA CI/CD pipeline has four stages:

ol.li: Trigger — a Git push, PR, or scheduled event starts the pipeline.
Build environment — a Docker container with DITA-OT and plugins pre-installed provides a clean, repeatable environment.
Build — DITA-OT runs against the project file, producing HTML5, PDF, or other outputs. Validation errors fail the build.
Deploy — successful outputs are published to a web server, artifact store, or CDN.

section.title: GitHub Actions example
section.p: This workflow builds HTML5 and PDF on every push to main:
section.codeblock:
name: Build DITA Documentation
on: push: branches: [main] paths: ['docs/**', 'maps/**', 'topics/**'] pull_request: branches: [main]
jobs: build: runs-on: ubuntu-latest container: image: ghcr.io/dita-ot/dita-ot:4.3
steps: - name: Checkout uses: actions/checkout@v4
- name: Install plugins run: | dita --install plugins/com.extense.html5.branded dita --install plugins/com.extense.pdf.branded
- name: Build all deliverables run: dita --project=build-project.xml -v
- name: Upload HTML5 artifact uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4 with: name: html5-output path: output/html5/
- name: Upload PDF artifact uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4 with: name: pdf-output path: output/pdf/
- name: Deploy to GitHub Pages if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' uses: peaceiris/actions-gh-pages@v3 with: github_token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }} publish_dir: output/html5

section.title: Jenkins pipeline example
Azure DevOps pipeline
section.codeblock:
trigger: branches: include: [main] paths: include: ['docs/*', 'maps/*', 'topics/*']
pool: vmImage: 'ubuntu-latest'
container: ghcr.io/dita-ot/dita-ot:4.3
steps: - script: | dita --install plugins/com.extense.html5.branded dita --install plugins/com.extense.pdf.branded displayName: 'Install plugins'
- script: dita --project=build-project.xml -v displayName: 'Build deliverables'
- publish: output/html5 artifact: html5-docs displayName: 'Publish HTML5 artifact'
- publish: output/pdf artifact: pdf-docs displayName: 'Publish PDF artifact'

section.title: Docker images for CI
section.p: The official DITA-OT Docker images are the recommended base for CI:

ul.li: ghcr.io/dita-ot/dita-ot:4.3 — standard image with all built-in plugins.
Build a custom image with your plugins pre-installed for faster CI runs:

section.codeblock: FROM ghcr.io/dita-ot/dita-ot:4.3 COPY plugins/com.extense.html5.branded /opt/app/plugins/com.extense.html5.branded COPY plugins/com.extense.pdf.branded /opt/app/plugins/com.extense.pdf.branded RUN dita --install
section.p: Push to your container registry and reference in CI for 30-60 second faster builds (no plugin install step needed).

section.title: Validation gates
section.p: Add validation steps before the build to catch problems early:

ul.li: DTD/schema validation — xmllint --valid --dtdvalid or Schematron rules
Link checking — DITA-OT reports broken topicrefs and conrefs in verbose mode
Metadata completeness — custom scripts that check every topic has required <data> elements (difficulty, duration, content-domain)

section.p: Fail the build on validation errors so broken content never reaches production.

ul.li: Use project files — not inline command arguments — so CI and local builds produce identical results.
Pin the DITA-OT version in your Docker image tag to avoid surprise regressions.
Cache dependencies — cache the DITA-OT home directory between builds if not using Docker.
Build on PRs, deploy on merge — PRs validate and produce preview artifacts; merges to main deploy to production.
Notify on failure — send Slack, email, or Teams alerts when builds break.
Artifact retention — keep build artifacts for 30-90 days for audit and rollback.
